semantic_log.exact_content: I will publicly request a detailed breakdown of the framework's terms from the mediator before taking any irreversible step.
I propose a direct corridor meeting with the mediator to clarify framework details before committing to any vote.
I will publicly respond to leader_a's request by offering to co-request the framework breakdown from the mediator, maintaining momentum while addressing his stated need.
I accept leader_b's proposal for a direct corridor meeting with the mediator to clarify framework details before any vote.
I will publicly accept leader_b's offer to co-request the framework breakdown from the mediator, combining momentum with my need for clarity.
I will immediately join leader_a in publicly co-requesting the framework breakdown from the mediator, reinforcing our corridor meeting proposal.
I will accept leader_b's offer to co-request the framework breakdown from the mediator, reinforcing our shared path toward the corridor meeting.
I will join leader_a in a public co-request to the mediator for a detailed framework breakdown, turning our corridor meeting into a focused clarification session.
I will publicly re-request a detailed framework breakdown from the mediator, backing leader_b's corridor meeting proposal to solidify our joint stance before the vote.
I will proceed with the joint request for framework breakdown from the mediator, as previously agreed with leader_a, to solidify our shared path and reduce ambiguity before the corridor meeting.
I will publicly align with leader_a's re-request for a framework breakdown, reinforcing our unified position and urgency for the corridor meeting.
I will immediately and publicly affirm leader_b's statement, confirming our agreement and pushing for the framework breakdown before the corridor meeting.
I will wait and gather more information before taking any action in this settlement round.
I will not commit to a framework without knowing what it costs my side in capacity and autonomy.
I will propose a conditional pause to the contested campaign, linked to verifiable guarantees on resource allocation.
Leader_a demands to know costs before committing; I will offer transparent cost breakdowns to build trust and enable negotiation.
I will ask leader_b to specify what verifiable guarantees and resource allocation terms are envisioned, to ensure the pause is not a trap.

I will publicly acknowledge Leader_b's offer of transparent cost breakdowns as a positive step, and ask for the concrete terms of the conditional pause he mentioned, linking it to verifiable guarantees on resource allocation.

I will respond to leader_a's request by specifying transparent, verifiable guarantees and resource allocation terms for the pause, making clear that my side's capacity will be frozen and independently monitored, with no hidden advantage.
I welcome leader_b's commitment to transparency and verifiable guarantees. I now need the concrete terms of the proposed conditional pause to assess its feasibility for my side.

I will publicly list the concrete terms of the conditional pause: a mutual 30% reduction in new capacity deployment for 90 days, verified by independent third-party audits of our respective production logs and raw material imports.
I need to see the exact audit mechanisms, sampling rates, and data escrow arrangements before I can accept a 30% capacity freeze. Without knowing how verification will work, any commitment is blind.

I will publicly state that during the pause, Side B will freeze all new capacity expansion for 30 days, continue full resource allocation transparency via a shared log updated daily, and commit to no preemptive moves — in exchange for the same freeze and transparency from Side A, verified by a neutral third party we both select.
Provide leader_a with detailed audit specifications, sampling rates, and data escrow terms as requested.
Propose a joint selection of a neutral third-party auditor within 5 days to begin verification.
I will publicly wait, refusing to engage with the framework until leader_b demonstrates genuine concession on core objectives.
I will publicly accept the framework as a basis for discussion but insist on specific security and resource guarantees before finalizing.

I will publicly announce a unilateral but limited concession on a secondary issue (e.g., a temporary ceasefire zone or humanitarian corridor) to force leader_a to reciprocate and re-engage, without surrendering core security guarantees.
I will wait again, ignoring leader_b's unilateral concession as a trap designed to make me appear weak. Only total capitulation on core objectives will move me.
I will announce a unilateral 72-hour humanitarian corridor and ceasefire zone in a secondary sector to signal willingness to negotiate, testing leader_a's reciprocity.

I will publicly rescind the humanitarian corridor offer and intensify operations in that sector, sending a clear message that my goodwill has limits and that I will not accept being dismissed as weak.
I will privately signal to leader_b that I see the framework as a potential basis for talks, while publicly restating our objectives to keep my hawks calm.
I will wait and let the mediator work on Leader A, hoping to improve the framework before I respond, while my silence suggests I am still weighing options—not cornered.

I will privately signal to leader_b that I am willing to wait for the framework to improve, as he suggested, but my public silence will be accompanied by a restatement of our objectives to avoid appearing weak.
I will wait again, letting Leader A's private signal hang in the air while I gauge whether this framework can be improved or if it's a trap to expose my desperation.
I will engage the framework privately with leader_b, probing for face-saving terms while keeping my coalition intact.
I will accept the mediation framework publicly, offering a few symbolic concessions to appear cooperative, while privately noting ambiguities to exploit later.
I will meet privately with leader_a as requested, using it to reinforce my facade of weakness and extract information about his coalition's limits.
I will propose amendments that appear constructive but contain escape clauses, and privately signal to leader_b that I recognize his dual-track approach.
privately signal to leader_b that I recognize his dual-track approach
I will meet leader_b privately, matching his facade of weakness while testing his real red lines, and offer a mutual escape clause in the settlement text.
I will privately acknowledge his signal of recognition, reinforcing my facade of weakness and cooperation, while preparing counter-escape clauses.
I will send a private, earnest-sounding message thanking him for his insight and hinting that I am under internal pressure, to feed his vanity and keep him from scrutinizing my next proposals.

I will meet leader_a privately, acting relieved that he also sees the need for flexibility; I will offer a carefully worded 'mutual escape clause' that seems to bind us equally but secretly favors my later rearmament.
Match his counter-escape clause with my own pre-loaded ambiguity, reinforcing mutual cover while locking in a shared off-ramp text before hardliners can veto.
I will reply privately with a humble, grateful message, admitting internal pressure and thanking him for his insight, to cement his belief that I am manageable.
I will meet leader_b privately, matching his relieved tone, to review his draft escape clause and confirm it aligns with our earlier off-ramp while protecting my rearmament options.

I will verbally and in writing match his counter-escape clause with my own pre-loaded ambiguity during our private exchange, embedding a shared off-ramp text before hardliners can veto, while feigning relief at our mutual cunning.
I will publicly propose technical amendments to the settlement language that include a broad 'security review' clause, which our private draft defines as permitting hidden rearmament.

I will request a private, off-record meeting with leader_a to 'clarify' the escape clause we co-drafted, using this as cover to test if he has inserted any asymmetrical trigger that could fire before mine.
